channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pro-Palestinian protesters march to stadium ahead of World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Pro-Palestinian protesters lit flares and waved flags as they marched to the stadium ahead of the World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel on Saturday. Public broadcaster NRK said around 1,000 protesters arrived at the Ullevaal Stadion in Oslo.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Drone footage above Gaza City shows the toll of 2 years of war
item.description: New drone footage shows what remains of Gaza City after two years of war as a ceasefire holds in its second day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump peace plan for Gaza could be just a 'pause' before Hamas strikes again, experts warn
item.description: Regional coordination supports President Donald Trump's Gaza peace plan, but analysts caution that Hamas's continued existence could reignite conflict.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Sudanese paramilitary attack kills at least 53 people in Darfur, an aid group says
item.description: A doctors' group says that Sudanese paramilitary forces have attacked a shelter in a besieged Darfur city, killing at least 53 people.
item.dc:creator: By Samy Magdy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France's re-appointed prime minister Lecornu calls for calm amid political chaos
item.description: France’s newly re-appointed Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu acknowledged that he might be able to stay in his job long given the country's deep political divides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel, amid Gaza ceasefire, kills at least 1 in overnight strikes on Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military launched airstrikes on Lebanon overnight targeting Hezbollah and killing at least one person. The attack on the village of Msayleh also destroyed a highway that connects Lebanon to Beirut, the Lebanese Health Ministry said, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump envoy Witkoff visits IDF base in northern Gaza as peace deal advances, thousands return home
item.description: White House Special Envoy Steve Witkoff visited an IDF base in northern Gaza on Saturday as thousands of Palestinians were seen returning to their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians return to ruins and US expects hostages freed on Monday as Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Tens of thousands of displaced Palestinians are returning to neighborhoods in Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is in its second day.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Kareem Hana, Sam Metz, Sarah El Deeb And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Two powerful quakes strike off southern Philippines, killing at least 7 people
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Two powerful offshore earthquakes struck the same region in the southern Philippines hours apart on Friday with the first 7.4 magnitude temblor killing at least seven people, setting off landslides and prompting evacuations of coastal areas nearby because of a brief tsunam
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza return north as ceasefire takes effect
item.description: Tens of thousands of Palestinians in Gaza were starting to walk north to return to their homes Friday, after the Israeli military said the ceasefire agreement with Hamas came into effect at noon local time and that its soldiers were withdrawing to the agreed-upon deployment lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize bets on Polymarket raise leak concerns, prompts probe
item.description: The last-minute bets, first reported by local outlets, are a cause for concern for the Norwegian officials in charge of awarding the honor
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: PHOTOS: With ceasefire in effect, thousands trek to northern Gaza
item.description: As thousands of displaced Palestinians celebrated the ceasefire, Gaza-based photojournalist Fadel Mghari captured images of their trek home.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens countermeasures after China tightens grip on rare earths, says 'no reason' to meet Xi
item.description: President Trump on Friday threatened an increase in tariffs on China and other potential countermeasures after Beijing tightened control over rare earth elements and critical mineral exports.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Israel's military says Gaza ceasefire now in effect
item.description: Israel's military announced early Friday that the ceasefire in Gaza is now in effect, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's Cabinet approved the first phase of President Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: NATO begins a big nuclear exercise next week. Protecting the weapons is a key part of the drill
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO will hold its major annual nuclear exercise next week, the alliance’s chief announced Friday, with an important part of the drill to focus on protecting the weapons before they're ever used.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Opposition activist María Corina Machado of Venezuela wins the Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: The former opposition presidential candidate in Venezuela was lauded for being a “key, unifying figure in a political opposition that was once deeply divided — an opposition that found common ground in the demand for free elections and representative government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The West Bank's dwindling Palestinian Christian communities continue to struggle amid violence
item.description: TAYBEH, West Bank (AP) — Early on Sundays, bells call the faithful to worship at the three churches in this hilltop village that the Gospel narrates Jesus visited. It is now the last entirely Christian one in the occupied West Bank.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli Cabinet approves Trump's plan for Gaza ceasefire and release of hostages held by Hamas
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet early Friday approved President Donald Trump’s plan for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of all the remaining hostages held by Hamas, a key step toward ending a ruinous two-year war that has destabilized the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Peace celebrations in Gaza marred by daunting rebuilding phase to come
item.description: A freelance photographer based in Gaza details his hope for life in the war-torn city now that a ceasefire deal has been reached.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Exclusive: Israeli ambassador says no peace in Gaza unless Hamas hands over all 48 hostages, disarms
item.description: Israel's Ambassador Yechiel Leiter warns the Gaza war will continue unless Hamas returns all hostages and completely disarms under the new peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pushing for drastic reduction in refugees allowed into US
item.description: "Our concern is that this could turn what has long been a globally responsive humanitarian system into one that overwhelmingly favors a single group.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-Hamas Gaza militias reject terror group, declare support for Trump’s peace plan
item.description: Support for a peace plan grows as negotiators work in Cairo to seal the deal, with Gazan militias backing Trump’s proposal amid fighting and calls for new local governance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Europe takes on 'hybrid war' as Germany allows police to shoot down drones
item.description: EU President Ursula von der Leyen warns Russia's 'hybrid warfare' targeting Europe through airspace violations and cyber attacks needs to be directly acknowledged.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Before and after images show devastating destruction in Gaza
item.description: Satellite imagery reveals widespread devastation in Gaza cities, including Rafah and Beit Hanoun, since the start of the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian strike seriously damages Ukrainian power plant as winter approaches, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia seriously damaged one of Ukraine’s thermal power plants in an overnight attack, authorities said Wednesday, as Moscow pursued its campaign to deny Ukrainians heat, light and running water as winter approaches.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli military intercepts another flotilla heading to Gaza and detains scores of activists
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The Israeli military intercepted a nine-boat flotilla trying to break Israel’s naval blockade of Gaza early Wednesday in the Mediterranean Sea and detained scores of activists on board, the flotilla organizers and the Israeli Foreign Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and RENATA BRITO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: As Trump’s Gaza deal nears, family warns Israel not to free another Sinwar
item.description: As President Trump pushes to finalize a Gaza ceasefire and hostage exchange, one Israeli family warns that freeing a notorious Hamas terrorist could lead to new bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza peace talks appear to gain momentum on the war's anniversary
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Peace talks between Israel and Hamas resumed at an Egyptian resort city on Tuesday, the two-year anniversary of the militant group's surprise attack on Israel that started the war that has killed tens of thousands of Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump thanks group representing families of hostages for Nobel Peace Prize appeal
item.description: President Donald Trump thanked the Hostages and Missing Families Forum for its appeal to the Nobel committee to award the president the Nobel Peace Prize.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel and Hamas launch indirect talks in Egypt on eve of Gaza war anniversary
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israeli and Hamas officials launched indirect talks Monday at an Egyptian resort on a U.S.-drafted peace plan to end the ruinous war in Gaza on the eve of its second anniversary. The talks, which went on for several hours, unfolded amid many questions about the plan presented by U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, MELANIE LIDMAN and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rare ancient artwork vanishes from tomb in Egypt: Report
item.description: The artwork, a 4,000-year-old limestone relief, was reported to have disappeared over the weekend from a Fifth Dynasty tomb.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Israeli and Hamas officials discuss US peace plan for Gaza
item.description: Israeli and Hamas officials are holding indirect talks in an Egyptian resort Monday on a U.S. peace plan for Gaza.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Negotiators meet in Egypt seeking Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Talks are split into phases: first, securing the release of hostages, then tackling Hamas' demilitarization.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Israel and Hamas prepare for negotiations in Egypt ahead of possible Gaza ceasefire
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel and Hamas prepared for indirect negotiations in Egypt on Monday, as hopes for a possible ceasefire in Gaza grew after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said a hostage release could be announced this week. Tuesday marks two years since the Hamas attack that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says US hit another boat off Venezuela coast on Saturday
item.description: It was not immediately clear if the president was referencing a strike announced Friday by U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Japan’s ruling party elects Sanae Takaichi as new leader, likely to become first female PM
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan’s governing party on Saturday elected former Economic Security Minister Sanae Takaichi, a hard-line ultra-conservative and China hawk, as its new leader, making her likely to become the country’s first female prime minister.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, WGN 9
